books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.desc: Tiny changes, remarkable results. A groundbreaking guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, incremental improvements.
books.longDesc: No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving every day. James Clear reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. If you're having trouble changing your habits, the problem isn't you — the problem is your system.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.desc: A landmark book exploring the two systems of the mind — the fast, intuitive System 1 and the slow, deliberate System 2.
books.longDesc: Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman explains the two systems that drive the way we think. System 1 is fast, intuitive, and emotional; System 2 is slower, more deliberative, and more logical. Kahneman reveals the pervasive influence of intuitive impressions on our thoughts and behavior, and shows how we can tap into both systems effectively.

books.title: The Lean Startup
books.author: Eric Ries
books.desc: How today's entrepreneurs use continuous innovation to create radically successful businesses through validated learning.
books.longDesc: Most startups fail. But many of those failures are preventable. The Lean Startup is a new approach being adopted across the globe, changing the way companies are built and new products are launched. Eric Ries defines a startup as an organization dedicated to creating something new under conditions of extreme uncertainty and shows how to navigate that challenge.

books.title: Sapiens
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.desc: A brief history of humankind — from the Stone Age to the 21st century, exploring how biology and history have defined us.
books.longDesc: 100,000 years ago, at least six human species inhabited the earth. Today there is just one. How did our species succeed? Why did our foraging ancestors come together to create cities and kingdoms? Harari examines how we came to believe in gods, nations and human rights, and how we came to be enslaved by bureaucracy, timetables and consumerism.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.desc: Rules for focused success in a distracted world — the ability to focus without distraction is becoming increasingly rare and valuable.
books.longDesc: Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. It's a skill that allows you to quickly master complicated information and produce better results in less time. Cal Newport shows how deep work is like a superpower in our increasingly competitive economy and provides a rigorous training regimen for cultivating this skill.

books.title: Man's Search for Meaning
books.author: Viktor E. Frankl
books.desc: A psychiatrist's memoir of surviving Nazi death camps and his discovery of logotherapy — the search for meaning as the primary human drive.
books.longDesc: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl's memoir has riveted generations of readers with its descriptions of life in Nazi death camps and its lessons for spiritual survival. Based on his own experience, Frankl argues that we cannot avoid suffering but we can choose how to cope with it, find meaning in it, and move forward with renewed purpose.

books.title: Zero to One
books.author: Peter Thiel
books.desc: Notes on startups, or how to build the future. Every moment in business happens only once — the next Bill Gates will not build an operating system.
books.longDesc: The great secret of our time is that there are still uncharted frontiers to explore and new inventions to create. Peter Thiel shows how we can find singular ways to create new things. He begins with the contrarian premise that we live in an age of technological stagnation, and charts a path toward a future of genuine progress and abundance.

books.title: The Power of Now
books.author: Eckhart Tolle
books.desc: A guide to spiritual enlightenment, teaching the importance of living in the present moment and transcending thoughts of the past and future.
books.longDesc: The Power of Now is a guide to spiritual enlightenment. Tolle's message is simple: living in the now is the truest path to happiness and enlightenment. He shows that our unhappiness is caused by our identification with our minds, and provides practical tools to break free from compulsive thinking and access a deeper state of consciousness.

books.title: Steve Jobs
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.desc: The exclusive biography based on over forty interviews with Steve Jobs — an intimate portrait of the creative entrepreneur.
books.longDesc: Based on more than forty interviews with Jobs conducted over two years — as well as interviews with more than a hundred family members, friends, adversaries, competitors, and colleagues — Walter Isaacson has written a riveting story of the roller-coaster life and searingly intense personality of a creative entrepreneur whose passion for perfection revolutionized six industries.

books.title: The 7 Habits
books.author: Stephen Covey
books.desc: Highly effective people share a commitment to principles — a framework for personal and professional effectiveness that has inspired millions.
books.longDesc: Stephen Covey's 7 Habits of Highly Effective People has captivated readers for 25 years. With penetrating insights and pointed anecdotes, Covey reveals a step-by-step pathway for living with fairness, integrity, honesty, and human dignity — principles that give us the security to adapt to change and the wisdom and power to take advantage of new opportunities.

books.title: Influence
books.author: Robert Cialdini
books.desc: The classic book on persuasion, explaining the six principles of influence and how they shape our decisions and behaviors.
books.longDesc: Dr. Robert Cialdini explains the psychology of why people say yes and how to apply these understandings. You'll learn the six universal principles of influence, how to use them to become a skilled persuader — and how to defend yourself against them. A must-read for anyone who wants to understand the science of compliance.

books.title: A Brief History of Time
books.author: Stephen Hawking
books.desc: An accessible account of cosmology, time, and the universe — a landmark work that made complex physics comprehensible to general readers.
books.longDesc: Was there a beginning of time? Could time run backwards? Is the universe infinite or does it have boundaries? Starting from basic ideas of space and time, Stephen Hawking goes on to explain black holes, the big bang, the nature of time, and a unification of physics — all written with remarkable clarity and wit for the non-scientist.
